text: This study analyzes Ahmad Syarif Yahya’s views on interreligious harmony through his works Ngaji Toleransi (The Preaching Of Tolerance) and Fikih Toleransi (Islamic Sharia For Tolerance). Islamic ideology with which the Muslims in Indonesia live nowadays is explained by Ahmad Syarif Yahya in relation to religion and national life. The study was initiated to respond to the modern era where international relations which often go hand in hand with diplomacy blur the boundaries between diplomacy and religion which cause an unstable atmosphere where society no longer knows the limits of religion as a private belief which needs to be hidden in the private domain to avoid confusion in the public domain. This study uses the descriptive – analytical method and literature study to make clear Ahmad Syarif Yahya’s concept of tolerance. According to the analysis, tolerance according to Ahmad Syarif Yahya has three components, namely maslahah (interests), universal fraternity and peace. In addition, Ahmad Syarif Yahya makes a clarification regarding the scope of Islamic tolerance in relation to the limits between syariah mutual relationship (muamalah) between the groups and the principles of the creed which must be kept safe from syncretism with other faiths outside of Islam. This study is hoped to contribute to Indonesian concept of Islamic moderation that is beneficial to establishing a pluralistic society based on solidarity of humanity with differences of opinions on faith kept intact.
